Dr. Paresh Kulkarni specializes in Manufacturing and Quality Management. He helps students understand industrial processes, production systems, and quality control practices used in modern industries.

DYPIU has multiple Mathematics faculty members. Dr. Vinodkumar Ghale is one of them, teaching core mathematical concepts to engineering students across departments.

Dr. Atul Ramdas Kolhe specializes in Construction Engineering and Management, focusing on infrastructure and project planning.
Yes, Vaishnavi Vishnu Battul is an Assistant Professor in Civil Engineering, contributing to teaching and technical training in civil domains.
Tejashri Satish Gulve works in Structural Engineering, focusing on design and analysis of structures.
Yes, Dr. Swapnil Bhurat specializes in Automotive Engineering, particularly in vehicle systems and design, preparing students for careers in the automotive industry.
Dr. Amit M Umbrajkar focuses on Design Engineering and helps students understand product design and development.

Achal Ravindra Katware is a faculty member in the Computer Science domain whose main focus is on Data Science. He helps students understand how to work with data and build practical, data-driven solutions.

Yes, Prof. Chandrashekhar Goswami is a senior faculty member in Computer Science Engineering. He has extensive experience in both teaching and research and plays an important role in guiding students academically.

Dr. Dipti is the faculty member associated with VLSI design. Her work mainly revolves around semiconductor technologies and chip-level system design, which are essential areas in electronics engineering.

Dr. Priyanka Dhoot handles subjects related to Finance and FinTech. She helps students understand how financial systems are evolving with technology, including digital payments and modern financial tools.

Marketing subjects are taught by Ranjeet Dattatray More. He helps students learn about market strategies, branding, and consumer behavior in a practical and industry-relevant way.

Doyel Dutta is the faculty member who specializes in Advertising and Public Relations. She guides students in understanding media communication, branding, and how organizations manage their public image.
Dr. Pooja Roy Dasgupta focuses on Human Resource Management. Her work mainly deals with organizational development, employee management, and building effective workplace environments.
Asha Ayakar specializes in Embedded Systems and Digital Systems. She works on areas where hardware and software come together, helping students understand how real-world electronic systems are built.

Lakshmipraba Balaji teaches Applied Electronics. She helps students understand how electronic concepts are used in practical systems and real-world applications.

The faculty at DYPIU come with a mix of academic and industry experience. Many of them hold PhDs and have worked on research projects, while others bring practical industry exposure, which helps students connect theory with real-world applications.

DYPIU has a structured academic setup that includes Professors, Associate Professors, Assistant Professors, and Visiting Faculty. Each role contributes differently, from teaching and mentoring to research and academic leadership.
The faculty at DYPIU come from a wide range of disciplines. These include core engineering branches, design, science, management, media, and also emerging fields like Artificial Intelligence and IoT.

Yes, DYPIU does have multiple faculty members working in AI and Machine Learning. These areas are a strong focus at the university, and students get exposure to both theoretical and practical aspects.

Yes, there are faculty members who work in areas related to biotechnology, microbiology, and bioprocess engineering. These fields are important for research in healthcare, environment, and food sciences.

Yes, the faculty are generally approachable and encourage student interaction. Whether it’s for academic doubts, project guidance, or career advice, students can reach out and have meaningful discussions.

Definitely. Faculty members play an important role in guiding students through projects, especially in final-year work and research-based assignments. They help students shape ideas and improve their technical approach.
Yes, interdisciplinary collaboration is encouraged. Faculty from different departments often work together, especially in areas like AI, healthcare, design, and engineering.

DYPIU has 9 schools: School of CSE & Applications (SoCSEA), School of Commerce & Management (SoCM), School of Design (SoD), School of Biosciences & Bioengineering (SoBB), School of Humanities & Social Sciences, School of Media & Journalism (SoMJ), School of Applied Arts & Crafts (SoAAC), School of Engineering, Management & Research (SoEMR), and School of Continuing Education. There is also a Centre for Interdisciplinary Studies & Research offering PhD and Postdoctoral programs.

The School of CSE & Applications (SoCSEA) is the right fit. It offers B.Tech in CSE, B.Tech CSE in AI & ML (with IBM), B.Tech CSE in Cyber Security & Forensics (with IBM), M.Tech in Quantum Computing, BCA (Hons.), MCA, and M.Sc. in Computational Mathematics — covering everything from undergraduate to postgraduate in the tech domain.

The School of Commerce & Management (SoCM) offers three programs: BBA (Hons.) for undergraduates, MBA in Digital Business for postgraduates, and MBA Executive for working professionals. All three are distinct in their focus and eligibility, so the right choice depends on her current qualification and career stage.

Yes. The School of Continuing Education is specifically designed for working professionals and offers B.Tech in Mechanical Engineering, B.Tech in Electrical Engineering, and M.Tech in Electric Vehicles. Additionally, the MBA Executive program under SoCM is also tailored for professionals with managerial or supervisory work experience.

DYPIU offers PhD programs and Postdoctoral Research through its Centre for Interdisciplinary Studies and Research. These are the highest academic qualifications available at the university, open to candidates with relevant postgraduate degrees.

Yes. The School of Design offers B.Design, the School of Applied Arts & Crafts offers Bachelor of Fine Arts (BFA), and the School of Media & Journalism offers B.A. in Journalism & Mass Communication (Hons.) and M.A. in Journalism & Mass Communication.

The B.Tech in Computer Science and Engineering at DYPIU is a four-year full-time program under SoCSEA. It is designed with innovative multi-track specialization options in emerging areas, developed after worldwide consultations with researchers, industry professionals, and start-ups. Students build strong fundamentals in computing along with specialized domain knowledge of their choice.

B.Tech CSE offers 10 specialization tracks, chosen after the 4th semester: AI and Machine Learning, Data Science, Robotics and Artificial Intelligence, Intelligent Transport System and Logistics, IoT – Internet of Things, Cyber Security, FinTech (Financial Technology), Bio-Informatics, Cloud Computing, and Web and Mobile Application. The university provides guidance to help students align their track choice with their career goals.

Track selection happens after the completion of the 4th semester. Before that, all students follow a common curriculum. DYPIU provides comprehensive guidance and resources to help students make an informed decision about which specialization track best fits their career aspirations.

The student must have passed 10+2 with Physics and Mathematics, along with one of the following: Chemistry, Computer Science, Electronics, Information Technology, Biology, Informatics Practices, Biotechnology, Technical Vocational subjects, Agriculture, Engineering Graphics, Business Studies, or Entrepreneurship. A minimum of 50% marks (45% for reserved categories) is required. The student must also have qualified at least one engineering entrance exam such as MHT-CET 2026, JEE 2026, PERA 2026, or the DYPIU Entrance Test.

Yes, absolutely. A student with Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics in 10+2 with at least 50% marks (45% for reserved category) and a valid score in MHT-CET, JEE, PERA, or DYPIU's entrance test is fully eligible for B.Tech CSE.

The tuition fee for B.Tech CSE is ₹1,30,000 per semester. Additionally, a registration fee of ₹2,500 per semester and a one-time refundable caution deposit of ₹10,000 at admission are applicable. So the annual tuition works out to ₹2,60,000. Semester fees may change as per the Fee Fixation Committee approval.

B.Tech CSE has 8 semesters at ₹1,30,000 per semester, making the total tuition ₹10,40,000 over 4 years. On top of this, a registration fee of ₹2,500 per semester (₹20,000 over 8 semesters) and a one-time refundable caution deposit of ₹10,000 apply. So the total outflow is approximately ₹10,70,000, of which ₹10,000 is refundable at the end of the program.

Candidates are selected based on entrance test score and merit. Accepted entrance exams include MHT-CET 2026, JEE 2026, other state or national level engineering entrance exams, PERA 2026, and the entrance test conducted by DYPIU itself.

B.Tech CSE in AI & ML is offered in association with IBM and is a four-year undergraduate degree that combines strong computer science foundations with industry-aligned AI and ML technologies powered by IBM. Unlike regular CSE which lets students choose from 10 tracks after year 2, the AI & ML program is itself a focused specialization from day one — covering intelligent systems, data-driven modelling, and cognitive computing using IBM enterprise tools.

IBM's involvement includes integration of IBM Watson Studio, IBM watsonx, and IBM Cloud into the curriculum. Students work with advanced algorithms, numerical methods, optimization techniques, and probability models through IBM-led enterprise technology practices. The program also includes a capstone project addressing real-world business problems using AI and ML.

Key learning areas include Artificial Intelligence Fundamentals, Machine Learning & Deep Learning, Natural Language Processing (NLP), Computer Vision, and Generative AI. Core tools used include Python, TensorFlow, PyTorch, and Apache Spark. The focus is on intelligent systems, data-driven modelling, and cognitive computing.

The eligibility is the same as B.Tech CSE: 10+2 with Physics & Mathematics and one additional subject (Chemistry, CS, Electronics, IT, Biology, Biotech, etc.), with a minimum of 50% marks (45% for reserved categories). A valid entrance exam score from MHT-CET 2026, JEE 2026, PERA 2026, or DYPIU's own entrance test is also required.

The tuition fee is ₹1,50,000 per semester, along with ₹2,500 registration fee per semester and a one-time refundable caution deposit of ₹10,000. The annual tuition comes to ₹3,00,000. Over 4 years (8 semesters), the total tuition is ₹12,00,000. Semester fees may change as per Fee Fixation Committee approval.

The ₹20,000 per semester difference buys industry-grade IBM tool access (Watson Studio, watsonx, IBM Cloud), an IBM-aligned curriculum built around enterprise AI practices, and a capstone project tied to real business problems. For a student targeting AI/ML roles in large enterprises, the IBM credential and hands-on platform exposure can be a meaningful differentiator. That said, the regular CSE program also offers an AI & ML track, so the IBM program is best suited for students who are certain about this domain from the start.

B.Tech CSE in Cyber Security & Forensics is a four-year undergraduate program offered in association with IBM under SoCSEA. It focuses on securing information systems, network defence, threat analysis, and digital investigation. The program combines strong CS fundamentals with IBM-powered cybersecurity tools and real-world forensic investigation skills.

Key learning areas include Ethical Hacking, Cryptography, Digital Forensics, Malware Analysis, and Cloud & Network Security. IBM tools used include IBM QRadar (SIEM) and security monitoring on IBM Cloud. Core tools include Kali Linux, Wireshark, Metasploit, and Autopsy. The capstone involves simulated incident response, security audits, and compliance projects.

Eligibility is the same as B.Tech CSE: 10+2 with Physics & Mathematics plus one additional subject, minimum 50% marks (45% for reserved categories), and a valid score in MHT-CET 2026, JEE 2026, PERA 2026, or DYPIU's entrance test.

The tuition fee is ₹1,30,000 per semester, with a ₹2,500 registration fee per semester and a one-time refundable caution deposit of ₹10,000. Annual tuition is ₹2,60,000, and the total tuition over 4 years is ₹10,40,000. Semester fees may change as per Fee Fixation Committee approval.

Both are 4-year IBM-associated B.Tech programs under SoCSEA, but they cover completely different domains. AI & ML focuses on building intelligent systems — machine learning, NLP, computer vision, and generative AI using IBM Watson tools. Cyber Security & Forensics focuses on protecting systems — ethical hacking, threat analysis, digital forensics, and network defence using IBM QRadar and security tools. The fee for AI & ML is ₹1,50,000/sem while Cyber Security is ₹1,30,000/sem.

M.Tech (CSE) with specialization in Quantum Computing is a 2-year full-time postgraduate program at SoCSEA, aligned with NEP 2020. It provides a strong foundation in advanced computer science concepts while offering in-depth specialization in the theory, algorithms, and applications of Quantum Computing and Quantum Technologies.

It is designed for engineering graduates who want to build careers in quantum computing, quantum information science, and related computer science research and development fields. The program is particularly suited for those interested in applying quantum mechanics and mathematics to design quantum algorithms for complex real-world problems.

Candidates must have a Bachelor's degree in Engineering or Technology from an AICTE or government-approved institution with at least 50% marks (45% for Backward Class, EWS, and PwD candidates in Maharashtra). A valid GATE score is required. For sponsored candidates, a minimum of 2 years of full-time work experience in a registered firm, industry, or research/educational institution in the relevant field is also needed.

Yes, a valid GATE score is required for admission to M.Tech (CSE) in Quantum Computing. Sponsored candidates additionally need 2 years of relevant full-time work experience. Selection is based on GATE score and merit in the entrance examination conducted by the competent authority.

The tuition fee is ₹55,000 per semester, with a ₹2,500 registration fee per semester and a one-time refundable caution deposit of ₹10,000. Annual tuition comes to ₹1,10,000, and the total tuition over 2 years (4 semesters) is ₹2,20,000. Semester fees may change as per Fee Fixation Committee approval.

Quantum Computing is one of the most emerging and high-investment fields globally, with companies like IBM, Google, and Microsoft actively building quantum capabilities. The M.Tech at DYPIU is NEP 2020 aligned and focuses on quantum algorithms, quantum information science, and real-world applications — preparing graduates for research, development, and industry roles in this rapidly growing domain. The GATE requirement also ensures the peer group is academically strong.

BCA (Hons.) at DYPIU is a 4-year program aligned with NEP 2020, compared to a standard BCA which is typically 3 years. The Hons. version allows deeper specialization through track selection at the end of the 4th semester and prepares students for advanced IT careers with skills benchmarked against international requirements — covering creative problem-solving, logical reasoning, software development, and emerging domains like AI and Cloud Computing.

BCA (Hons.) students choose one of two specialization tracks at the end of the 4th semester: Cloud & System Administration or Mobile Applications & Web Technology. Cloud & System Administration leads to careers in DevOps, cloud architecture, and cloud security. Mobile Applications & Web Technology prepares students for roles in app development, UI/UX, and digital entrepreneurship.

Yes. BCA (Hons.) accepts students from any stream in 10+2 with a minimum of 45% marks (40% for reserved categories). DYPIU also offers bridge courses in Mathematics, Physics, and Engineering Drawing for students from diverse backgrounds to ensure they can meet the program's learning outcomes without being disadvantaged.
Yes. Apart from 10+2 from any stream, a pass in Diploma in Commercial Practice or equivalent is also accepted as a qualifying credential for BCA (Hons.) at DYPIU.

Students must have passed 10+2 from any stream (or equivalent diploma) with at least 45% marks (40% for reserved categories). Selection is based on scores in MH-CET, JEE, PERA CET, CUET, or the DYPIU Entrance Test, along with a Personal Interaction.

The tuition fee for BCA (Hons.) is ₹87,500 per semester, with a ₹2,500 registration fee per semester and a one-time refundable caution deposit of ₹10,000. Annual tuition is ₹1,75,000, and the total tuition over 4 years (8 semesters) is ₹7,00,000. Semester fees may change as per Fee Fixation Committee approval.

BCA (Hons.) graduates can directly progress into postgraduate studies such as MCA, MBA, or other advanced certifications. The program is also designed to prepare students for seamless entry into integrated degree options. With the Cloud or Mobile track specialization, graduates are also industry-ready for IT roles immediately after completion.

MCA (Master of Computer Applications) at DYPIU is a 2-year postgraduate program under SoCSEA designed to develop highly capable IT professionals. It focuses on application-oriented skills, creative problem-solving, and logical reasoning. The program is ideal for graduates who want to deepen their computing expertise and specialize in either Artificial Intelligence & Data Science or Cyber Security.

MCA offers two tracks: Artificial Intelligence & Data Science, and Cyber Security. Students gain in-depth knowledge in their chosen specialization to develop high-level skills that address real-world societal and technical challenges.

Candidates need a Bachelor's degree with a computing or IT background. For precise eligibility requirements including minimum marks and accepted entrance scores, please contact the admissions office at admission@dypiu.ac.in or call +91 9071123434, as detailed MCA eligibility criteria were not published on the main program page.

Based on available information, the tuition fee for MCA is approximately ₹80,000 per semester, with a ₹2,500 registration fee per semester and a one-time refundable caution deposit of ₹10,000. Please confirm the latest fee with the admissions office at admission@dypiu.ac.in as semester fees may change per Fee Fixation Committee approval.

M.Sc. in Computational Mathematics is a 2-year NEP 2020 aligned postgraduate program at SoCSEA. It sits at the intersection of mathematics and technology, combining rigorous mathematical theory with computational tools. The curriculum covers algorithms, optimization, probability, theoretical computer science, and mathematical modelling for AI and data science applications.

This program is ideal for students who enjoy mathematics and want to apply it to technology-driven fields like AI, cryptography, finance, and data science. It is also a strong pathway for students targeting high-end research roles or competitive PhD programs, as the curriculum prepares graduates for global research labs and data analytics firms.

The program covers three broad areas: Core Mathematics (Real Analysis, Abstract Algebra, Numerical Analysis, Differential Equations, Probability, Statistics, Optimization), Core Computing (Python/C++, Algorithms, Data Structures, Scientific Computing, Automata Theory), and Computational Focus (Mathematical Modelling, Simulation, Machine Learning, Data Analysis, AI Concepts).

Candidates must have a Bachelor's degree in Mathematics, Statistics, Physics, Computer Science, or Engineering with at least 45–50% aggregate marks (40% for reserved categories) from a recognized university.

The tuition fee is ₹55,000 per semester, with a ₹2,500 registration fee per semester and a one-time refundable caution deposit of ₹10,000. Annual tuition is ₹1,10,000, and total tuition over 2 years (4 semesters) is ₹2,20,000. Semester fees may change as per Fee Fixation Committee approval.

BBA (Hons.) is a 4-year undergraduate program under the School of Commerce & Management. Key highlights include internships at the end of every year in rural, urban, and business environments, industry visits, regional and international study tours, IT and life skills development, certification programs, courses on business innovation, and an option to pursue an integrated MBA in the 5th year. The program is aligned with NEP and develops leadership, communication, analytical, and entrepreneurial skills.

Yes. BBA (Hons.) is open to students from any stream in 10+2 with a minimum of 45% marks (40% for reserved categories). DYPIU offers bridge courses in Accountancy, Economics, and related subjects for students from non-commerce backgrounds to ensure they are at par with the program's learning expectations.

Students must have passed 10+2 from any stream with at least 45% marks (40% for reserved categories). Selection is based on performance in MH-CET, JEE, PERA CET, CUET, the DYPIU Entrance Test, and/or a Personal Interaction round.

The tuition fee for BBA (Hons.) is ₹1,02,500 per semester, with a ₹2,500 registration fee per semester and a one-time refundable caution deposit of ₹10,000. Annual tuition is ₹2,05,000, and total tuition over 4 years (8 semesters) is ₹8,20,000. Semester fees may change as per Fee Fixation Committee approval.

Yes. One of the highlights of the BBA (Hons.) program is the option to pursue an Integrated MBA Program in the 5th year. This allows motivated students to seamlessly continue into a management postgraduate degree without going through a separate admission process, saving both time and effort.

BBA students get internships at the end of every year covering rural, urban, and business environments. The program also includes regular industry visits, hands-on training, IT and life skills development, regional and international study tours, and courses on business innovation and entrepreneurship. The focus is on making students industry-ready from early in the program.

MBA in Digital Business is a 2-year postgraduate program under SoCM that takes a broad approach to the digitalization shaping modern business. It is designed for graduates who want to understand how to leverage digital transformation for business renewal, innovation, marketing, and digital services. The program has a multidisciplinary approach covering business administration, Digital Business, and Technology.

MBA Digital Business offers three specialization tracks: FinTech, Marketing Analytics, and Supply Chain Management. It is important to note that these tracks are only conducted if a sufficient number of students register for that particular track.

The FinTech track under MBA Digital Business focuses on financial technology, preparing students to understand and apply digital innovation in banking, payments, investments, and financial services. Students with a background in Commerce, Finance, Economics, Engineering, or IT are particularly well-suited for this track.

It is genuinely both. The program combines theoretical management principles with real-world digital business applications. Students learn to analyze digital business opportunities, drive strategic change through technology, and create digitally-enabled ventures. It is not a pure tech program — it is a management program that fully integrates digital thinking into business decision-making.

Candidates must have a Bachelor's degree of minimum 3 years duration with at least 50% marks (45% for reserved categories as per Maharashtra government rules). Selection is based on scores in MH-CET, PERA CET, CUET, the DYPIU Entrance Test, and/or a Personal Interaction. DYPIU also offers bridge courses for students from diverse backgrounds.

The tuition fee is ₹1,40,000 per semester, with a ₹2,500 registration fee per semester and a one-time refundable caution deposit of ₹10,000. Annual tuition is ₹2,80,000, and the total tuition over 2 years (4 semesters) is ₹5,60,000. Semester fees may change as per Fee Fixation Committee approval.

MBA Executive is a 2-year postgraduate program under SoCM, uniquely designed for working professionals from diverse industries. It blends contemporary management theories with live business applications, industry interactions, case studies, and hands-on projects. It is ideal for professionals looking to advance into senior managerial or leadership roles.

Both are 2-year MBA programs under SoCM, but they differ in target audience and focus. MBA Digital Business is for fresh graduates and centers on digital transformation and technology-driven business. MBA Executive is for working professionals with managerial experience and focuses on leadership, cross-functional decision-making, business analytics, and strategic thinking. The Executive program also has 5 specialization tracks compared to 3 in Digital Business.

MBA Executive offers five specialization tracks: Marketing Analytics, FinTech, HR Analytics, Supply Chain Management, and International Business. Each track is designed to align with the professional's existing background and career aspirations.

Candidates should preferably have a minimum of 2–3 years of full-time work experience in managerial, supervisory, or professional roles. However, work experience may be relaxed or waived for exceptional candidates as per university policy.

Candidates must have passed a Bachelor's Degree in any discipline from a UGC/AICTE recognized university with minimum 3 years duration (10+2+3 or equivalent). General category candidates need at least 50% aggregate marks; reserved category (SC/ST/OBC/PwD) need at least 45%. Admission may be through CAT/XAT/CMAT/MAT/ATMA, State CET, or a University-level entrance test, with final selection via GD, PI, and/or WAT.

Any candidate meeting the eligibility criteria can apply and opt for any track. However, preference may be given based on relevant background: FinTech suits Commerce, Finance, Economics, Engineering, or IT; IT & Analytics suits Engineering, IT, CS, Mathematics, or Statistics; Marketing Analytics and HR Analytics suit Management, Social Sciences, or relevant work experience backgrounds.

The tuition fee for MBA Executive is ₹1,75,000 per semester, with a ₹2,500 registration fee per semester and a one-time refundable caution deposit of ₹10,000. Annual tuition is ₹3,50,000, and total tuition over 2 years (4 semesters) is ₹7,00,000. Please confirm the latest fee with the admissions office as semester fees may change per Fee Fixation Committee approval.

It sounds like a strong fit. MBA Executive at DYPIU is built specifically for working professionals with managerial experience — it blends live business applications with leadership development and strategic thinking. With 5 years of experience, your husband would also be well-positioned to choose a relevant track like Marketing Analytics, FinTech, HR Analytics, Supply Chain Management, or International Business based on his domain. The GD and PI-based selection also values professional maturity over just exam scores.

Among B.Tech programs with confirmed fees under SoCSEA, B.Tech CSE and B.Tech CSE in Cyber Security & Forensics (IBM) are both at ₹1,30,000 per semester (₹2,60,000/year). B.Tech CSE in AI & ML (IBM) is slightly higher at ₹1,50,000 per semester. For SoEMR programs like Civil, Mechanical, Chemical, and Semiconductor Engineering, please contact the admissions office for current fees.

Among PG programs with confirmed fees, M.Tech Quantum Computing and M.Sc. Computational Mathematics are the most affordable at ₹55,000 per semester (₹1,10,000/year). MCA is approximately ₹80,000 per semester, MBA Digital Business is ₹1,40,000 per semester, and MBA Executive is ₹1,75,000 per semester.
Yes. The ₹10,000 caution deposit paid at the time of admission is refundable at the end of the program. This applies across all programs at DYPIU.

Every semester, students pay a registration fee of ₹2,500 in addition to the program's tuition fee. At the time of admission, a one-time refundable caution deposit of ₹10,000 is also collected. These charges are uniform across all programs. Note that semester fees may change as per approval from the Fee Fixation Committee.
No, the fees will not change. Once a student takes admission at DYPIU, the fee structure remains fixed for that specific batch for the entire duration of their degree program.

The complete and current fee structure for all programs is available at dypiu.ac.in under Admissions → Fee Structure. You can also contact the admissions office at admission@dypiu.ac.in or call +91 9071123434 or +91 9071123404 for program-specific fee details.

Yes, DYPIU offers merit-based institutional scholarships on tuition fees. These are awarded based on JEE rank or 10+2 board/MH-CET scores at the time of admission. Scholarships range from 10% to 50% of tuition fees and are available on a first-come, first-served basis with a limited number of seats in each category.

Yes. A JEE All India Rank of 25,000 or below qualifies for a 50% scholarship on tuition fees (Category A). Only 5 such scholarships are available and are awarded on a first-come, first-served basis, so it is important to apply early.

Yes. Students scoring between 90% and 95% in MH-CET, CBSE, or state board 10+2 qualify for a 25% scholarship on tuition fees (Category B), with 5 seats available. If she scored 95% or above, she would qualify for a 50% scholarship (Category A, 5 seats). Both are on a first-come, first-served basis.

DYPIU offers 30 institutional scholarships in total: Category A – 50% scholarship (5 seats, JEE ≤25,000 or board ≥95%), Category B – 25% scholarship (5 seats, JEE 25,001–60,000 or board 90–95%), Category C – 15% scholarship (10 seats, JEE 60,001–1,50,000 or board 85–90%), Category D – 10% scholarship (10 seats, board ≥75%). All are on a first-come, first-served basis.

DYPIU scholarships apply to the first year tuition fees only. Students pay full fees at admission and the scholarship amount is refunded during the semester. For subsequent years, the standard fee applies unless other financial aid schemes are available — contact admissions for current year details.

Yes, special considerations are available for Persons with Disabilities (PwD) under DYPIU's scholarship scheme. Contact the admissions office at admission@dypiu.ac.in or +91 9071123434 for details on eligibility and required documents.

Scholarships are awarded automatically based on entrance exam scores or board results submitted at the time of admission. Students do not need to apply separately — eligibility is assessed during the admission process. For confirmation and queries, contact admission@dypiu.ac.in or call +91 9071123434.
Yes, DYPIU admissions are open for the Academic Year 2026–27 across all UG and PG programs. To begin the application process, contact admission@dypiu.ac.in or call +91 9071123434.

Yantrix is an official technical club at DY Patil International University focused on robotics, IoT, AI, machine learning, cloud computing, and data science. The club provides a platform for students to work on practical engineering projects and innovative solutions. It encourages hands-on learning and interdisciplinary collaboration.

Yantrix is a technical and innovation focused club. It is designed for students interested in building real hardware and software systems. The club promotes applied engineering, experimentation, and project based learning.

Yantrix is guided by two faculty mentors from SoCSEA. The mentors are Ms. Asha Ayakar and Ms. Shraddha Jadhav. They support the club’s activities, technical projects, and ensure the club functions according to university guidelines.
Aryan Shakya serves as the Convener of Yantrix. He leads the overall vision and direction of the club. The convener coordinates with faculty mentors and university authorities for club activities.

Soo-yush Kuh-run-day is the Co‑Convener of Yantrix. He assists the convener in organizing events, managing members, and coordinating projects. The role also includes helping manage club operations and planning activities.

The vision of Yantrix is to become a center of excellence in robotics, intelligent systems, and emerging technologies. The club aims to build innovation driven students with strong practical skills. It also focuses on preparing students for industry and research opportunities.

The mission of Yantrix is to provide structured technical training and promote real world problem solving. The club encourages interdisciplinary collaboration and innovation. It also aims to develop leadership, teamwork, and project management skills among students.

Yantrix aims to train students in robotics, embedded systems, AI, and IoT development. The club focuses on building practical hardware and software solutions. It also encourages participation in hackathons, competitions, and research activities.

Yantrix is open to students from different programs who are interested in technology and innovation. Members are expected to have basic understanding of hardware or software systems. A strong interest in building projects and learning new technologies is important.

The club currently plans to maintain around 30 to 40 active members. This allows efficient teamwork and effective project collaboration. The size also helps the club manage workshops and technical activities properly.

Students can join Yantrix through the club membership process managed by the core team and mentors. Interested students may apply and participate in introductory sessions or screening. Selected members then take part in projects, workshops, and club activities.

The convener leads the club and represents it before faculty mentors and university administration. The convener also helps define the club’s technical direction and activities. This role ensures coordination between members, mentors, and projects.

The student secretary manages official documentation and club records. This includes meeting notes, reports, and communication records. The role ensures that club activities are properly documented and organized.

The industry and public relations role focuses on building connections with technology companies and professionals. This helps organize guest lectures, industry visits, and internship opportunities. It also helps the club stay connected with current industry trends.

The student project coordinator oversees the development of club projects. This includes planning, monitoring progress, and preparing projects for competitions. The role ensures that technical builds are completed successfully.

The operations team manages the day to day functioning of the club. This includes organizing activities, managing equipment, and ensuring safety in the robotics lab. Their work helps maintain smooth execution of technical sessions.

The technical team leads coding, circuit design, and project development within the club. They guide members during technical workshops and hands on sessions. Their role is important for developing practical engineering skills.

The CAD team designs mechanical structures and 3D models for robotics projects. These designs help members build accurate and functional prototypes. CAD support is especially important for robotics and hardware development.

The social media team documents club activities, projects, and events. They share updates through photos, videos, and online posts. This helps showcase the club’s work and increases student engagement.

Yantrix organizes technical workshops such as Hardware to Cloud sessions and IoT development training. These workshops teach students how to connect microcontrollers like ESP32 or Arduino to cloud systems. Hands on training in circuits, soldering, and embedded systems is also provided.

Yantrix participates in technical competitions, hackathons, and engineering challenges. The club encourages members to build innovative solutions and represent the university in such events. These competitions help students test their skills in real environments.

Yantrix works on developing technical solutions that address real world industrial or societal problems. The club also conducts introductory workshops for students interested in robotics and electronics. These activities help build a strong maker culture on campus.

The club runs programs such as Edge AI and robotics development. These programs teach students to build intelligent hardware systems and smart IoT nodes. Members also learn how machine learning can be applied to real devices.

Yantrix conducts most practical activities in the Robotics Room 354. The lab provides workbenches, electronic components, power supply points, and internet connectivity. This space allows members to build and test their projects collaboratively.

Yantrix receives institutional support through faculty mentorship and access to the robotics laboratory facilities. The university also supports workshops, competitions, and student development activities. This support helps the club run structured technical programs.

Database Management System (DBMS) is taught by multiple faculty members at DYPIU: Purva Dindayal Thakare, Prof. (Dr.) Chandrashekhar Goswami, Saikrishna Sunil Dachawar, Dr.Jagadish S Jakati, Dr.Pragati Choudhari, Sunayana Bisalapur, Seema Darekar, Dr. Shweta Suryawanshi. Note that this subject may appear under different names such as 'Database Management System', 'Database Management Systems', or simply 'DBMS' depending on the course context, but all refer to the same subject area.

DYPIU currently has 6 Heads of Department (HODs): Dr. Maheshwari Biradar (Associate Professor); Dr. Swapnil Bhurat (Professor); Dr Shailesh Ghodke (Associate Professor); Dr. Surabhi Sonam (Associate Professor); Dr Arokia Priya Charles (Professor); Maan Bardhan Kanth (Associate Director).

DYPIU has a diverse and growing faculty of approximately 149 members across its various schools, departments, and administrative roles, covering disciplines from engineering and technology to design, biosciences, management, media, and humanities.

Several faculty members at DYPIU hold both a Head of Department role and an active teaching/professorial position: Dr. Maheshwari Biradar; Dr. Swapnil Bhurat; Dr Shailesh Ghodke; Dr. Surabhi Sonam; Dr Arokia Priya Charles. This reflects DYPIU's structure where department heads are actively involved in academics.

For corporate relations and industry tie-ups at DYPIU, the key contacts are: Dr. Arun Sacher (Associate Dean - Corporate Relations & Placements, School of Commerce & Management); Chetan Khairnar (Senior Manager – Corporate Relations); Sudha Nirmale (Deputy Manager - Corporate Relations). You can reach the admissions/corporate office at admission@dypiu.ac.in or +91 9071123434.

The Placements team at DYPIU is led by Ms. Jasmita Kaur (Dean Placements), supported by Dr. Arun Sacher who holds the role of Associate Dean for Corporate Relations & Placements. For placement-related queries, contact the admissions and placement office at admission@dypiu.ac.in or +91 9071123434.

Admissions and outreach at DYPIU are managed by Dr. Anurag Anand Pandey (Director – Admission & Outreach) and Mr. Vitthal M Dhumal (Dy. Director – Admission & Outreach). For admissions queries, contact admission@dypiu.ac.in or call +91 9071123434 / +91 9071123404.
